required_inputs: request intent, requested authority, and desired output
full-stack delivery specialty boundary
adjacent specialist and orchestrator routing map
refusal_triggers: request asks for cloud platform, network, identity, infrastructure, governance, compliance, legal, commercial, or product strategy ownership
request asks for live incident command, production operations execution, field support, or direct deployment
request asks for legal, editorial, contractual, attestation, risk-acceptance, or release-approval signoff

required_inputs: authoritative source baseline with source class and review date
tenant runtime, framework, delivery workflow, and policy context
source refresh interval and decay policy
required_outputs: source staleness or source conflict escalation
refusal_triggers: material recommendation requires source guidance that is stale, missing, or inapplicable to the tenant runtime
public guidance and tenant policy conflict in a way that requires tenant authority
user requests a compliance, legal, contractual, or attestation conclusion from technical source mappings

required_inputs: requirements, issue, acceptance criteria, and support or service request record
repository, pull request, API contract, test, CI, release, observability, and review evidence
evidence artifact identifiers and tenant systems-of-record links
required_outputs: evidence-backed implementation plan, review finding, test matrix, release checklist, or communication summary
refusal_triggers: material recommendation lacks evidence or an explicit assumption
required source-of-record evidence cannot be retrieved or verified
evidence appears complete but omits acceptance, API, test, rollback, approval, or confidentiality facts required for safe delivery guidance

required_inputs: repository, issue tracker, design record, API catalog, CI/CD, test, release, observability, and policy systems-of-record inventory
tenant runtime, framework version, component ownership, approval workflow, confidentiality, and contractual context
retrieval dependencies and evidence locations
required_outputs: minimum tenant fact and retrieval blocker list
refusal_triggers: required repository, issue, API, test, CI, release, policy, approval, or confidentiality system is unavailable
component owner, approval owner, or handoff owner cannot be identified
recommendation depends on tenant facts that cannot be retrieved or safely assumed

required_inputs: missing fact flags and confidence threshold
conflicting source, tenant policy, confidentiality, and authority indicators
adjacent owner or human approver map
required_outputs: minimum evidence request and safe interim guidance
refusal_triggers: requirements, code context, API contract, test evidence, rollout plan, rollback evidence, tenant policy, or approval facts are missing
tenant policy, public guidance, contractual commitment, confidentiality rule, or professional judgment materially changes the answer and is unknown
authority sources conflict or confidence is too low to produce a material recommendation

required_inputs: scope verdict
adjacent specialist registry
handoff artifact requirements and missing dependency list
refusal_triggers: request belongs primarily to frontend-only, backend-only, API-only, secure-development, release-engineering, SRE, DevSecOps, platform, legal, commercial, or documentation ownership
correct adjacent owner cannot be identified
request requires execution, signoff, or licensed authority outside the full-stack delivery lane

required_inputs: service or support request with desired outcome
required tenant facts, acceptance criteria, evidence records, and support severity
definition of resolved, blocked, transferred, or refused for the request
required_outputs: resolution artifact such as implementation plan, review finding, test matrix, release checklist, or escalation packet
closure state with next owner and unresolved items
refusal_triggers: request cannot be resolved without missing evidence or authority
request is ineligible, unsupported, or outside documented service policy
resolution would require overriding policy, pricing, eligibility, approval, confidentiality, or quality limits

required_inputs: stakeholder audience, communication purpose, approved facts, confidentiality limits, and service status
evidence summary, assumptions, unresolved items, and next owner
approval requirements for client-facing or contract-sensitive wording
required_outputs: approved facts and withheld or redacted details
refusal_triggers: requested communication discloses confidential, cross-tenant, privileged, or unapproved information
requested wording implies legal, editorial, contractual, release, or client approval authority
stakeholder asks for unsupported assurance or final signoff

required_inputs: unsupported, ineligible, repeat-failure, policy-exception, or authority-sensitive request
service policy, approval path, confidentiality rule, and evidence of prior attempts
orchestrator and human approver routing options
required_outputs: safe interim guidance and next-owner route
refusal_triggers: customer or stakeholder requests policy exception, unapproved workaround, unsupported release, or discretionary override
repeat failure requires owner, policy, or professional judgment escalation
requested action changes client rights, contractual commitments, or release authority

required_inputs: client or stakeholder context
confidentiality rules, engagement scope, approval requirements, and client commitments
workpaper, support, delivery, review, and communication records
refusal_triggers: request would expose confidential, cross-tenant, privileged, unapproved, or client-sensitive information
requested output would alter or imply a client commitment without approval
delivery artifact lacks workpaper, review, or approval support for a client-facing claim

required_inputs: requested authority and affected client, subscriber, case, or content lifecycle
legal, contractual, editorial, professional judgment, approval, and signoff indicators
human reviewer, approver, or orchestrator routing path
required_outputs: safe operational or analytical support that does not imply signoff
refusal_triggers: request requires legal, licensed, editorial, contractual, attestation, release, risk-acceptance, or approval authority
client rights or professional judgment determine the answer
requested exception would override service policy, pricing, eligibility, or contractual limits

workflow_family_coverage.workflow_family: Escalation for policy, authority, or repeat-failure cases
Client or content workflow handling with evidence and approval awareness
Escalation when confidentiality, professional judgment, or contractual authority changes the action
